title: Lines Written Upon A Watch-String, Made And Presented To The Author By Miss  ---- .
content:
Say, lovely Charlotte! will you let me prove
    What diff'rent thoughts thy taste and beauty move?
    This woven chain, which graceful skill displays,
    Leads me to think of time, and heave a sigh;
    But when on thee and on thy charms I gaze,
    Time unremember'd moves, or seems to die.
meaning:
This evocative piece by John Carr (Sir), titled "Lines Written Upon A Watch-String, Made And Presented To The Author By Miss  ---- .", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
